section_title: INTRODUCTION TO E-MINI S&P FUTURES CONTRACT AND ROLLOVER
content:
Welcome to the ICT 2022 Free Mentorship on YouTube. This episode focuses on the E-mini S&P futures
contract for June 2022.
- Current context: We are in the month of May, nearing the end of trading for the June contract.
- Contract Rollover: In a couple of weeks, trading for the June contract will end, and we will roll over into the
September 2022 contract. There are still a couple of weeks left for trading June.

- IDENTIFYING DISCOUNT AND OVERSOLD CONDITIONS
On the hourly chart, we are observing a swing low to a swing high, establishing our trading range.
Equilibrium is the midpoint of this range.
- Price Action: The market has dropped down into a discount and a fair value gap.
- Oversold Condition: This movement indicates an oversold condition. No indicator is needed to determine
this; the price action itself shows it.
- Tools: The rectangle visually highlights the specific area for the viewer. The Fibonacci tool is used to
identify equilibrium; anything above it is premium, anything below it is discount.
- Conclusion: The market has moved from a high down into a fair value gap and below equilibrium, placing
it in a discount condition.

- INITIAL PRICE ACTION AND LIQUIDITY SWEEPS
On the five-minute chart, we observed an initial run up in price.
- Premium Movement: I highlighted on Twitter that the market was likely to go higher for a premium relative
to the defined high and low.
- Aggressive Decline: After moving into a premium, the market slid aggressively, taking out relative equal
lows, which represents sell-side liquidity.
- 9:30 AM Open: This liquidation happened quickly right at the 9:30 AM opening.
- Second Liquidity Sweep: A short bounce was followed by another drop below the low, clearing out sell
- side liquidity for anyone attempting to capture a long position, effectively knocking them out.
- Market Structure Shift: Subsequently, a shift in market structure occurred, leading to a higher rally and
rebalancing of the previous aggressive drop.

section_title: ANTICIPATING HIGHER PRICES AND DRAWING ON LIQUIDITY
content:
The market then dropped back down into a five-minute fair value gap, which was not traded into.
- Price Prediction: I mentioned on Twitter that we would likely trade higher and take out the short-term high.
- Draw on Liquidity: The morning high was identified as the draw on liquidity.
- Rally Extension: The market rallied, going a little higher than initially anticipated for the morning, ultimately
reaching a fair value gap representing a very deep premium from the low to the high.
- Subsequent Pullback: After hitting the premium, price pulled back inside the range between the recent
low and high.

- PERSONAL TRADE EXECUTION AND LOGIC
Zooming into the one-minute chart, I executed a small trade in the morning.
- First Trade: I recorded this trade, sharing a small vignette on Twitter. I took partials at the time of posting
the tweet, and the limit order was filled.
- Second Trade: My only other trade for the day involved waiting for the market to drop down into a
discount. When it did, I went long.
- Risk Management: I experienced minimal 'heat' (about five handles), which was bearable. My stop loss
was placed just below the preceding swing low.
- Liquidity Logic: The market initially ran higher, then dropped to take out sell stops. I was buying those sell
stops. After the rally, I anticipated continued higher movement because the sell stops had been cleared out.

section_title: DETAILED MARKET STRUCTURE SHIFT AND FAIR VALUE GAP ENTRY
content:
Let's look at the one-minute chart in more detail, focusing on the initial drop.
- Market Structure Shift (SMS): We observed a short-term shift in market structure when the price took out
a preceding high.
- Entry Point: I bought a fair value gap that formed after this SMS. I did not expect the lows to be taken out
because it didn't occur during the 8:30 AM news event.
- Price Action Sequence: This was a deep retracement, followed by a rally into a premium just before 9:30
AM, then a sharp decline.
- Recognizing Traps: There was no clear model entry during the aggressive drop, indicating it was rushing
to clear out sell-side liquidity. It rallied, suckering in longs, then knocked them out again.
- Retail Psychology: Retail traders typically do not re-enter after being stopped out due to fear.
- Subsequent Setup: After this, price rallied, came down to a fair value gap, showed another shift in market
structure, rallied to relative equal highs, then dropped back into a very good order block.
- Final Liquidity Sweep: Price rallied, went into an imbalance, consolidated, bumped just above a short
- term high, then dropped one more time, clearing out remaining liquidity. My entry was at 4120 on five
contracts during this final liquidity sweep.

My trade details, including the entry at 4120 on five contracts, are verified via a tweet.
- Tweet Reference: The link to the tweet, with its time and date stamp, will be provided in the video
description. The tweet stated, 'Sell side liquidity is taken, free to run to 43 and a half.' This refers to the level
for a draw on buy-side liquidity.
- Partial Profit Taking: The first partial (three contracts) was sold at 4139.25, as confirmed in the tweet's
timeline.
- Final Exit: The final two contracts were filled on a limit order at 4143.75. The market ultimately went a little
higher, which was acceptable given administrative tasks being juggled.

This week is the first week of the month, transitioning between months, and includes a Non-Farm Payroll
(NFP) scenario.
- Personal Rule: I typically aim to complete all my trading by Wednesday.
- Satisfaction: Based on the trades demonstrated, I am satisfied with my performance for this particular
week.
- Discipline and Consistency: Maintaining discipline and consistency is crucial, which is achieved through
strict rules and engagement.
- Halting Trading: I will not seek additional trades this week because the daily chart does not provide a
clear definitive objective; price could go higher or lower. I choose to be content with what has been shown.
- Upcoming Content: I will be finishing work with my private group and will be quiet on Twitter and YouTube
until Thursday, when another video will be posted.
